jobs.description:
Your new company
Our client is a well‑established and leading sports publisher with a loyal consumer audience and an expanding digital footprint. They produce high-quality content across web, print and events, and are now investing in new revenue streams, subscription models, and technology-led experiences. They are recognised for delivering trusted, insight‑led sports journalism and engaging storytelling that resonates with fans across multiple generations.
With continued investment in digital innovation, they are evolving their platforms to enhance audience experience and deepen community engagement. This is an exciting opportunity to join a brand undergoing meaningful growth, where marketing plays a central role in shaping its future direction.
Please note: this role does not offer sponsorship.
Your new role
As a Marketing Executive, you will play a key role in driving B2C audience engagement, digital acquisition and subscription growth across multiple channels. This is a hands-on, varied role that suits someone confident managing multiple workstreams, comfortable with data, and excited to work across emerging digital tools and platforms.
You’ll be joining at an exciting point of growth as the brand diversifies into new revenue areas including subscription products and trade shows. This role offers real autonomy, visibility across the business, and the chance to influence marketing strategy.
.
Key responsibilities include:
- Deliver digital marketing campaigns across email, social, paid and web to support audience growth and subscriptions.
- Analyse campaign performance, user behaviour and engagement metrics to inform optimisation and reporting.
- Support subscription marketing and retention activity, helping develop new customer journeys and lifecycle programmes.
- Contribute to marketing around trade shows, consumer events and brand activations.
- Manage content updates, newsletters and CMS activity in collaboration with editorial teams.
- Work across new digital tools and emerging technologies to enhance customer experience.
- Work independently to manage deadlines, juggle multiple tasks and adapt quickly to changing priorities.
What you'll need to succeed
:
- Experience within publishing is essential.
- Strong digital marketing skills (email, social, analytics, CMS).
- A data-driven mindset with the ability to interpret and act on insights.
- Confidence working autonomously and managing several projects at once.
- Curiosity for new technologies and an eagerness to test, learn and optimise.
- A proactive, “joiner” attitude — someone who enjoys contributing broadly within a lean team.
- This role would suit a marketer with approximately 3–6 years’ experience, looking to step into a position with more ownership and variety.
What you'll get in return
- Opportunity to work across diverse campaigns in a fast‑moving, creative environment
- Exposure to senior stakeholders
- Hybrid working
- Supportive, collaborative team culture
- Excellent scope for professional development and progression
- Salary £35,000–£40,000 depending on experience.
- Opportunity to learn, develop and shape marketing activity for a respected sports publisher.

jobs.description:
I’m delighted to partner with an innovative construction business that’s growing fast and are now looking to appoint a B2B Senior Marketing Campaign Executive to join their team.
If you’re a creative marketer who enjoys working with technical products and knows how to turn complex ideas into clear, compelling stories, this role is for you!
If you’re the kind of marketer who loves owning campaigns end-to-end, turning complex technical detail into clear, compelling content, and proving impact through leads, conversion and performance optimisation — this role is for you!
You’ll operate as a senior member of a small marketing team, taking full ownership of campaign delivery, performance, and continuous optimisation — and you’ll see the commercial results of your work in action.
About the role.
You’ll manage integrated campaigns from planning → execution → optimisation → reporting, with clear commercial outcomes:
- Build and deliver multichannel campaigns across digital, email, social, and print
- Create bold, persona-led messaging that converts
- Translate technical product information into clear, engaging marketing content
- Own website messaging and conversion: update/optimise via CMS using SEO + UX best practice
- Partner closely with Sales, Business Development and Technical teams to align marketing with the pipeline.
- Produce high-quality content (case studies, blogs, email sequences, video)
- Design and edit assets using Adobe Suite / Canva and video tools

- Run campaigns and reporting through HubSpot (automation, workflows, journeys, performance)
The Candidate
We’re looking for a proven campaign owner — someone who can show results, not just activity:
- Minimum 5+ years’ experience managing integrated campaigns in a technical B2B product environment (manufacturing/industrial/engineering), ideally construction
- Strong grasp of lead gen funnels, buying journeys and conversion optimisation
- Hands-on channel experience: SEO, PPC, email, LinkedIn
- Experience with marketing automation/CRM (HubSpot strongly preferred)
- Confident turning complex technical detail into commercially effective marketing
- Comfortable managing multiple campaigns and priorities at pace
What success looks like in this role
- HubSpot campaigns and workflows run confidently and independently.
- Website performance improves measurably (traffic quality, time on page, conversion actions)
- Campaign reporting clearly shows what’s working, by channel and audience.
- A steady flow of qualified leads aligned to pipeline growth
What you’ll get
- Competitive salary DOE + monthly bonus
- 31 days holiday
- Buy/sell holiday scheme
- Free lunch everyday
- Birthday voucher
- Pension scheme
- Health insurance scheme (incl. discounted gym membership/appointment paybacks)
- Full-time on-site role with flexible start/finish times
If you’re ready to shape bold campaigns, elevate digital journeys, and bring fresh ideas to a growing team, we’d love to hear from you.
Early applications are strongly encouraged.

As DTC & Marketplace Manager, you’ll own and develop the performance of the company’s DTC ecommerce site and Amazon Seller account, with responsibility for revenue, profitability and efficiency.

jobs.description:

The team is growing fast, backed by the worlds top‑tier investors, and trusted by major global financial institutions who rely on the company's technology to operate with greater speed, precision, and insight. With a culture built around creative excellence, fast iteration, and ambitious product vision, this is a rare opportunity to shape and elevate the brand story of a category‑defining platform.
Your new role
As the Content Marketing Manager, you will take full ownership of the end‑to‑end content engine – from concept to execution to distribution. This is a hands‑on, high‑autonomy role where your creative instincts, editorial judgment, and ability to deliver quickly will directly shape how the company communicates with clients, investors, and the wider market.
You will:
